title: festuca trichophylla subsp. scabrescens
intro: Festuca trichophylla subsp. scabrescens, also known as alpine meadow-grass, is a type of grass species in the Poaceae family, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Festuca trichophylla subsp. scabrescens include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations. Traditionally, this plant has been prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
